title: Planet Labs Stock Surges 18% On Significant Revenue Beat In Fiscal Q3
text:
Planet Labs delivered a significant revenue beat in fiscal Q3 2026, with the stock surging 18% following the earnings announcement on December 10, 2025. The company reported Q3 revenue of $81.3 million, representing 33% year-over-year growth and exceeding analyst expectations. The strong performance demonstrated accelerating momentum in the company's core business segments.

Analysts project the company's fiscal 2027 revenue of $415 million to $440 million, indicating roughly 39% growth ahead. This projection underscores confidence in the company's ability to capitalize on its expanding backlog and growing demand from defense and government customers. Planet Labs' business model, centered on subscription-based data delivery with more than 90% of revenue coming from recurring subscriptions, provides visibility and stability for future growth.

The company achieved non-GAAP gross margins of 58% in Q3, maintaining the discipline that helped achieve its first full year of non-GAAP profitability. The $900 million contract backlog, predominantly from defense and government customers, positions the company to deliver sustained growth. Major contract wins include multi-year agreements with Sweden's Armed Forces and continued expansion of government contracts across multiple nations.

Investors responded positively to the combination of strong Q3 results, robust forward guidance, and the strategic momentum in defense and intelligence markets. The 18% stock surge reflected market enthusiasm for Planet Labs' transformation into a profitable, growth-oriented space technology company.
